[Qadaa](/wiki/Qadaa "Qadaa") | [district](/wiki/Districts_of_Iraq "Districts of Iraq") | [Subdistricts of Iraq](/wiki/Subdistricts_of_Iraq "Subdistricts of Iraq")

* [Mintaqah](/wiki/Mintaqah "Mintaqah") (منطقة region) * [Qadaa](/wiki/Kaza "Kaza") (قضاء district) * Nahiyah (ناحية subdistrict) * [Markaz](/wiki/Markaz_(administrative_division) "Markaz (administrative division)") (مركز district) * [Mutamadiyah](/wiki/Mutamadiyah "Mutamadiyah") (معتمدية "delegation") * [Daerah](/wiki/Daerah "Daerah")/[Daïra](/wiki/Da%C3%AFra "Daïra") (دائرة circle) * [Liwa](/wiki/Sanjak "Sanjak") (لواء banner / *[sanjak](/wiki/Sanjak "Sanjak")*)

* [Amanah](/wiki/Amanah_(administrative_division) "Amanah (administrative division)") (أمانة municipality) * [Baladiyah](/wiki/Baladiyah "Baladiyah") (بلدية municipality) * Ḥai (حي neighborhood / quarter) * [Mahallah](/wiki/Mahallah "Mahallah") (محلة) * [Qarya](/wiki/Village "Village") (قرية) * [Sheyakhah](/wiki/Sheyakhah "Sheyakhah") (شياخة "neighborhood subdivision") * [Majlis Mahalliyy](/wiki/Local_council_(Israel) "Local council (Israel)") (مجلس محلي local council) * [Majlis ʾIqlimiyy](/wiki/Regional_council_(Israel) "Regional council (Israel)") (مجلس اقليمي regional council)

[newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Nahiyah%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Nahiyah%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Nahiyah%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Nahiyah%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(December 2021)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

([Arabic](/wiki/Arabic_language "Arabic language"): نَاحِيَة [[ˈnaːħijah]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Arabic "Help:IPA/Arabic"), plural نَوَاحِي, *nawāḥī* [[naˈwaːħiː]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Arabic "Help:IPA/Arabic")), also **nahiyeh**, **nahiya** or **nahia**, is a regional or local type of [administrative division](/wiki/Administrative_division "Administrative division") that usually consists of a number of villages or sometimes smaller towns. The  [Ottoman *nahiye*](/wiki/Nahiye_(Ottoman) "Nahiye (Ottoman)"), also called a *[bucak](/wiki/Bucak_(administrative_unit) "Bucak (administrative unit)")*, was a third-level or lower  [administrative division](/wiki/Administrative_divisions_of_the_Ottoman_Empire "Administrative divisions of the Ottoman Empire"), and remains as such in some [successor states](/wiki/Successor_state "Successor state") such as [Syria](/wiki/Syria "Syria"), [Iraq](/wiki/Iraq "Iraq"), [Lebanon](/wiki/Lebanon "Lebanon") and [Jordan](/wiki/Jordan "Jordan"), with the [Balkan](/wiki/Balkans "Balkans") states of [Serbia](/wiki/Serbia "Serbia") and [Montenegro](/wiki/Montenegro "Montenegro") having preserved the term for a while after [liberation](/wiki/History_of_the_Balkans#Rise_of_nationalism_in_the_Balkans "History of the Balkans") for the highest administrative unit as [*nahija*](/wiki/Nahiye_(Ottoman)#Successor_states_in_the_Balkans "Nahiye (Ottoman)"). In [Tajikistan](/wiki/Tajikistan "Tajikistan") and the  [autonomous Chinese region](/wiki/Autonomous_regions_of_China "Autonomous regions of China") of [Xinjiang](/wiki/Xinjiang "Xinjiang"), both from the [Turco-Persian](/wiki/Turco-Persian_tradition "Turco-Persian tradition") or  [Turkic](/wiki/Turkic_peoples "Turkic peoples") regions of [Asia](/wiki/Asia "Asia"), it is a second- and third-level division, respectively. A *nahiyah* can constitute a division of a *[qadaa](/wiki/Qadaa "Qadaa")*, *[mintaqah](/wiki/Mintaqah "Mintaqah")* or other such [district](/wiki/District "District")-type division and is sometimes translated as "[subdistrict](/wiki/Subdistrict "Subdistrict")".

Selçuk Akşin Somel. "Kazâ". *The A to Z of the Ottoman Empire*. Volume 152 of A to Z Guides. Rowman & Littlefield, 2010. p. 151. [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [9780810875791](/wiki/Special:BookSources/9780810875791 "Special:BookSources/9780810875791")

["Jamkarān"](https://www.iranicaonline.org/articles/jamkaran), Encyclopædia Iranica - online version. Quote: "... Jamkarān was the first village founded in the Qom district (*nāḥia*) by Jam. " Retrieved 15 Dec 2024.

["Census: i. In Iran"](https://www.iranicaonline.org/articles/census-i), Encyclopædia Iranica - online version. Quote: "The country was divided into 25 census regions (*nāḥīa*)..." Retrieved 15 Dec 2024.
["Āstāna"](https://www.iranicaonline.org/articles/astana-district-of-lahian-gilan), Encyclopædia Iranica, Vol. II, Fasc. 8, p. 837 - online version. Retrieved 15 Dec 2024.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Type of administrative division in West and Central Asia

"Nahiyeh" redirects here. For the villages in Iran, see [Nahiyeh](/wiki/Nahiyeh_(disambiguation) "Nahiyeh (disambiguation)").

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This article **needs additional citations for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this article](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Nahiyah "Special:EditPage/Nahiyah") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["Nahiyah"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Nahiyah%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Nahiyah%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Nahiyah%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Nahiyah%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Nahiyah%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Nahiyah%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(December 2021)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

A **nāḥiyah** ([Arabic](/wiki/Arabic_language "Arabic language"): نَاحِيَة [[ˈnaːħijah]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Arabic "Help:IPA/Arabic"), plural نَوَاحِي, *nawāḥī* [[naˈwaːħiː]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Arabic "Help:IPA/Arabic")), also **nahiyeh**, **nahiya** or **nahia**, is a regional or local type of [administrative division](/wiki/Administrative_division "Administrative division") that usually consists of a number of villages or sometimes smaller towns. The  [Ottoman *nahiye*](/wiki/Nahiye_(Ottoman) "Nahiye (Ottoman)"), also called a *[bucak](/wiki/Bucak_(administrative_unit) "Bucak (administrative unit)")*, was a third-level or lower  [administrative division](/wiki/Administrative_divisions_of_the_Ottoman_Empire "Administrative divisions of the Ottoman Empire"), and remains as such in some [successor states](/wiki/Successor_state "Successor state") such as [Syria](/wiki/Syria "Syria"), [Iraq](/wiki/Iraq "Iraq"), [Lebanon](/wiki/Lebanon "Lebanon") and [Jordan](/wiki/Jordan "Jordan"), with the [Balkan](/wiki/Balkans "Balkans") states of [Serbia](/wiki/Serbia "Serbia") and [Montenegro](/wiki/Montenegro "Montenegro") having preserved the term for a while after [liberation](/wiki/History_of_the_Balkans#Rise_of_nationalism_in_the_Balkans "History of the Balkans") for the highest administrative unit as [*nahija*](/wiki/Nahiye_(Ottoman)#Successor_states_in_the_Balkans "Nahiye (Ottoman)"). In [Tajikistan](/wiki/Tajikistan "Tajikistan") and the  [autonomous Chinese region](/wiki/Autonomous_regions_of_China "Autonomous regions of China") of [Xinjiang](/wiki/Xinjiang "Xinjiang"), both from the [Turco-Persian](/wiki/Turco-Persian_tradition "Turco-Persian tradition") or  [Turkic](/wiki/Turkic_peoples "Turkic peoples") regions of [Asia](/wiki/Asia "Asia"), it is a second- and third-level division, respectively. A *nahiyah* can constitute a division of a *[qadaa](/wiki/Qadaa "Qadaa")*, *[mintaqah](/wiki/Mintaqah "Mintaqah")* or other such [district](/wiki/District "District")-type division and is sometimes translated as "[subdistrict](/wiki/Subdistrict "Subdistrict")".

## Ottoman Empire

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nahiyah&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Ottoman Empire")]

The nahiye ([Ottoman Turkish](/wiki/Ottoman_Turkish_language "Ottoman Turkish language"): ناحیه) was an administrative territorial entity of the [Ottoman Empire](/wiki/Ottoman_Empire "Ottoman Empire"), smaller than a *[kaza](/wiki/Kaza "Kaza")*. The head was a *[mütesellim](/wiki/M%C3%BCtesellim "Mütesellim")* (governor) who was appointed by the [Pasha](/wiki/Pasha "Pasha").

The *kaza* was a subdivision of a *[sanjak](/wiki/Sanjak "Sanjak")*[[1]](#cite_note-Somel151-1) and corresponded roughly to a city with its surrounding villages. *Kaza*s, in turn, were divided into *nahiye*s (each governed by a *müdür*) and villages (*karye*, each governed by a *muhtar*).[[2]](#cite_note-Cetinsaya-2) Revisions of 1871 to the administrative law established the *nahiye* (still governed by a *müdür*) as an intermediate level between the kaza and the village.[[2]](#cite_note-Cetinsaya-2)

### Successor states in the Balkans

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nahiyah&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Successor states in the Balkans")]

After achieving [national liberation](/wiki/Wars_of_national_liberation "Wars of national liberation"), the [Principality of Serbia](/wiki/Principality_of_Serbia "Principality of Serbia") (1817–1833) and [Principality of Montenegro](/wiki/Principality_of_Montenegro "Principality of Montenegro") (1852–1910) preserved the term as *nahija* ([Serbian Cyrillic](/wiki/Serbian_Cyrillic_alphabet "Serbian Cyrillic alphabet"): нахија).

## Examples

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nahiyah&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Examples")]

### Arabic-speaking countries

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nahiyah&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Arabic-speaking countries")]

| Country | Level above (Arabic) | Level above (English) | Main article |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [Syria](/wiki/Subdivisions_of_Syria "Subdivisions of Syria") | [mintaqah](/wiki/Mintaqah "Mintaqah") (formerly qadaa) | [district](/wiki/Districts_of_Syria "Districts of Syria") |  |
| [Palestine](/wiki/Subdivisions_of_Palestine "Subdivisions of Palestine") | [Liwa'](/wiki/Liwa%27 "Liwa'") | [governorates](/wiki/Governorates_of_Palestine "Governorates of Palestine") | [Districts of Palestine](/wiki/Districts_of_Palestine "Districts of Palestine") |
| [Iraq](/wiki/Subdivisions_of_Iraq "Subdivisions of Iraq") | [Qadaa](/wiki/Qadaa "Qadaa") | [district](/wiki/Districts_of_Iraq "Districts of Iraq") | [Subdistricts of Iraq](/wiki/Subdistricts_of_Iraq "Subdistricts of Iraq") |
| [Lebanon](/wiki/Subdivisions_of_Lebanon "Subdivisions of Lebanon") |  |  |  |
| [Jordan](/wiki/Subdivisions_of_Jordan "Subdivisions of Jordan") | [Liwa'](/wiki/Liwa%27 "Liwa'") | [governorate](/wiki/Governorates_of_Jordan "Governorates of Jordan") | [Nahias of Jordan](/wiki/Nahias_of_Jordan "Nahias of Jordan") |

### Turkic-speaking territories

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nahiyah&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Turkic-speaking territories")]

* [Xinjiang, China](/wiki/Xinjiang "Xinjiang"): a subdivision of a [prefectural](/wiki/Prefectures_of_the_People%27s_Republic_of_China "Prefectures of the People's Republic of China").
* [Ottoman Empire](/wiki/Administrative_divisions_of_the_Ottoman_Empire "Administrative divisions of the Ottoman Empire"): subdistrict, commune, parish; a subdivision of a *[kaza](/wiki/Kaza "Kaza")* (قضاء).

### Other

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nahiyah&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Other")]

* [Districts of Tajikistan](/wiki/Districts_of_Tajikistan "Districts of Tajikistan"): a subdivision of a [province](/wiki/Provinces_of_Tajikistan "Provinces of Tajikistan").
* [Nahiye (Ottoman)#Successor states in the Balkans](/wiki/Nahiye_(Ottoman)#Successor_states_in_the_Balkans "Nahiye (Ottoman)")

## Persian language

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nahiyah&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Persian language")]

[Persian](/wiki/Persian_language "Persian language") has borrowed the Arabic word with the spelling ناحیه. [Encyclopædia Iranica](/wiki/Encyclop%C3%A6dia_Iranica "Encyclopædia Iranica") transliterates it mostly as *nahia* or, with diacritics, *nāḥia/nāḥīa*.[[3]](#cite_note-3) In modern contexts it may be used with the meaning of anything between 'census region',[[4]](#cite_note-4) and 'section' as in "Section (*nāḥia*) 2 of eleven local fishing stations".[[5]](#cite_note-5)
